title: podman ps occasionally hangs
body:
### Issue Description

After a while of running, `podman ps` seems to hang.

```
podman version
Client:        Podman Engine
Version:       5.8.1
API Version:   5.8.1
Go Version:    go1.26.1
Git Commit:    c6077f645788743258a1a749f8005b4fb3cbe533
Built:         Thu Mar 12 05:55:05 2026
Build Origin:  pkginstaller
OS/Arch:       darwin/arm64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.8.1
API Version:  5.8.1
Go Version:   go1.25.7 X:nodwarf5
Git Commit:   c6077f645788743258a1a749f8005b4fb3cbe533
Built:        Wed Mar 11 10:00:00 2026
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Start Podman and run a base image
2. After a while, VSCode will report "reconnecting to devcontainer" and it never does
3. `ps auxww | grep podman` and `kill` both of those PIDs
4. Restart Podman Desktop
5. In vscode `Reopen folder` and you'll be back connected to the devcontainer

### Describe the results you received

When calling `Podman ps` it hangs

### Describe the results you expected

When calling `Podman ps` it doesn't hang

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Hi @agardnerIT, thanks for reporting this issue.

To help us narrow down the root cause, could you provide some additional information?
When does the hang occur?
- Does it happen after your Mac wakes from sleep, or after a period of idle time?
- Or does it happen while you're actively working in the devcontainer?
- Roughly how long does Podman need to be running before it hangs? (minutes, hours, overnight?)

What happens during the hang?
- Does `podman ps` eventually return with an error, or does it hang forever until you kill it?
- When `podman ps` hangs, does podman machine ssh also hang, or can you still reach the VM?
- Debug logs: If you can catch it next time, could you run with debug logging and share the output?

```
podman --log-level=debug ps 2>&1 | tee podman-ps-hang.log
```
If it hangs, leave it for ~30 seconds, then `Ctrl+C` and share the `podman-ps-hang.log`. The last log line before it stops will tell us exactly where the request is stuck.

Also, could you share the gvproxy log from when the hang occurred?
```
cat "$TMPDIR/podman/gvproxy.log"
```

Devcontainer setup:
- Is this a single container or a pod (e.g., `docker-compose`)?
- Are there any port forwards or health checks configured?

Thanks!

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).

I'm facing the same problem on macOS 26.4.1 and Podman 5.8.2. I'm new to macOS and Podman but I'll try to answer your questions as good as possible. ``` $ podman info OS: darwin/arm64 buildOrigin: pkginstaller provider: applehv version: 5.8.2 ``` - Does it happen after your Mac wakes from sleep, or after a period of idle time? - Seems not related to sleep in my case. Also happens after a reboot. - Or does it happen while you're actively working in the devcontainer? - Usually happens to me while I'm actively working. I notice it when the integrated terminal in VS Code becomes unresponsive or when I can't open/save files anymore. - Roughly how long does Podman need to be running before it hangs? (minutes, hours, overnight?) - Varies widely. Sometimes I can work for hours without disruptions, sometimes it starts to hang just 5-10min after running `podman machine start` - Does podman ps eventually return with an error, or does it hang forever until you kill it? - Not sure. I haven't tried waiting more than a few mins because of high CPU usage and temperature (see screenshot at the end) **Debug output:** ``` $ podman --log-level=debug ps 2>&1 | tee podman-ps-hang.log time="2026-05-07T00:28:04+02:00" level=info msg="podman filtering at log level debug" time="2026-05-07T00:28:04+02:00" level=debug msg="Called ps.PersistentPreRunE(podman --log-level=debug ps)" time="2026-05-07T00:28:04+02:00" level=debug msg="SSH Ident Key \"/Users/my-user/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/machine\" SHA256:+ ssh-ed25519" Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: ssh: handshake failed: EOF time="2026-05-07T00:32:11+02:00" level=debug msg="Shutting down engines" ``` The gvproxy log does not seem to exist: `cat: /var/folders/qc/.../T/podman/gvproxy.log: No such file or directory` **Devcontainer setup:** - Is this a single container or a pod (e.g., docker-compose)? - Single container - Are there any port forwards or health checks configured? - No **Additional info:** When Podman starts to hang, the CPU usage goes up quite a bit. I need to run `podman machine stop` to fix it: ``` $ podman machine stop WARN[0090] Failed to gracefully stop machine, performing hard stop Machine "podman-machine-default" stopped successfully ```
Many thanks in advance!
It seems that Podman is missing a timeout for the HTTP client, and SSH dial discards the caller's context, so these are the likely causes. I will try to fix that.
